prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Ahoy, matey! Ye be askin' why the sky be blue? Well, ye see, the fair sky be reflectin' the color of the mighty ocean, which be a deep blue. When the sun shines down upon us, its light be scatterin' in the air, like a thousand shinin' jewels in the sky. The color blue be scatterin' more than other hues, so it be what our eyes spy. It be the cheeky game of Mother Nature, paintin' the canvas of the heavens with the color of the sea. So, the sky be blue to remind us of the vast, blue ocean that be the lifeblood of every pirate! Arrr!
